title: Life expectancy gap for Black Bostonians is growing, health officials warn
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The life expectancy gap between Black residents and others in Boston doubled from 3.3 to 6.6 years since 2013.
Black Bostonians have a life expectancy of 76.2 years; the average is 82.2 years for other residents, per a recent report.
Boston announced $2 million in investments for health organizations, emphasizing a partnership with Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.

title: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet calls out Hawks over ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet urged the Hawks to cancel their 'Magic City Night' promotion.
Kornet criticized the event for not mentioning Magic City is a strip club, raising concerns about objectification of women.
The promotion includes a performance by rapper T.I. and serving of Magic City’s lemon pepper wings at the Hawks' March 16 game.

title: Married couple from R.I. identified as victims in fatal Swansea crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Carlolyn Carcasi, 54, and James Carcasi, 53, from Bristol, Rhode Island, were killed in a Swansea crash on Feb. 27.
The crash involved an Infiniti driven by Demitri Sousa, 28, who allegedly shot a man in Rhode Island hours earlier.
The crash occurred at Route 136 and Route 6; both vehicles suffered catastrophic damage, and the Subaru was engulfed in flames.

title: Steve Kerr apologizes for comments about Kristaps Porzingis’ lingering illness
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Steve Kerr apologized for discussing Kristaps Porzingis' health condition, acknowledging his lack of expertise on medical matters.
Kristaps Porzingis has played only 18 games this season due to illness and fatigue, recently diagnosed with POTS.
Porzingis was recently traded to the Golden State Warriors, playing one game since joining, scoring 12 points in a loss.

title: Taking stock of Bruins’ top trade chips entering the NHL deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins evaluating trade options including draft picks and prospects before March 6 NHL trade deadline.
Draft picks, including Toronto's 2026 first-rounder, are considered valuable trade chips for Bruins' roster strategy.
Players like Mason Lohrei, Andrew Peeke, and others could be leveraged for potential roster upgrades.

title: NFL insider: Maxx Crosby trade could happen ‘this week’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots among several teams interested in Raiders' Maxx Crosby, potentially facing a trade this week.
Raiders receiving interest for Crosby, despite strong ties to owner and team; trade could happen organically.
Crosby frustrated with losing seasons and wants to win; trade possible before new league year on March 11.

title: Quincy man sentenced in fatal St. Patrick’s Day assault in downtown Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sanusi Sadiq, 31, of Quincy, sentenced to 3-6 years for manslaughter in the death of Barry Whelan.
Barry Whelan, 46, died from a traumatic brain injury after an assault on St. Patrick's Day 2023 in Boston.
A retrial found Sadiq guilty; previous trial ended in mistrial due to disputes over evidence from medical testimony.

title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Bill Simmons predicts Jayson Tatum will return on Friday for the Celtics' game against the Mavericks at TD Garden.
The Celtics ruled out Tatum for Tuesday's game against the Bucks, continuing his recovery from achilles tendon repair.
ESPN reports Tatum has been cleared for 5-on-5 practice; Celtics yet to announce an official return date.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court will hear arguments about allowing those under 21 to carry handguns.
Gun-rights advocates argue that the current age restriction violates the Second Amendment after the 2022 SCOTUS decision.
Federal law restricts licensed handgun sales to those under 21, and Massachusetts law currently restricts carrying handguns to over 21.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney aims to add reinforcements before the NHL trade deadline without sacrificing long-term plans.
Sweeney avoids 'win-now' rentals, focusing instead on securing long-term assets to bolster the team's depth.
Sweeney has no regrets sacrificing future assets for past championship runs, emphasizing a renewed focus on long-term goals.
